net.DEU_20_4: for the Lord your God goes with you to fight on your behalf against your enemies to give you victory.”
net.DEU_20_5: Moreover, the officers are to say to the troops, “Who among you has built a new house and not dedicated it? He may go home, lest he die in battle and someone else dedicate it.
net.DEU_20_6: Or who among you has planted a vineyard and not benefited from it? He may go home, lest he die in battle and someone else benefit from it.
net.DEU_20_7: Or who among you has become engaged to a woman but has not married her? He may go home, lest he die in battle and someone else marry her.”
net.DEU_20_8: In addition, the officers are to say to the troops, “Who among you is afraid and fainthearted? He may go home so that he will not make his fellow soldier’s heart as fearful as his own.”
net.DEU_20_9: Then, when the officers have finished speaking, they must appoint unit commanders to lead the troops.
net.DEU_20_10: When you approach a city to wage war against it, offer it terms of peace.
net.DEU_20_11: If it accepts your terms and submits to you, all the people found in it will become your slaves.
net.DEU_20_12: If it does not accept terms of peace but makes war with you, then you are to lay siege to it.
net.DEU_20_13: The Lord your God will deliver it over to you, and you must kill every single male by the sword.
net.DEU_20_14: However, the women, little children, cattle, and anything else in the city—all its plunder—you may take for yourselves as spoil. You may take from your enemies the plunder that the Lord your God has given you.
net.DEU_20_15: This is how you are to deal with all those cities located far from you, those that do not belong to these nearby nations. Laws Concerning War with Canaanite Nations
net.DEU_20_16: As for the cities of these peoples that the Lord your God is going to give you as an inheritance, you must not allow a single living thing to survive.
net.DEU_20_17: Instead you must utterly annihilate them —the Hittites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites —just as the Lord your God has commanded you,
net.DEU_20_18: so that they cannot teach you all the abhorrent ways they worship their gods, causing you to sin against the Lord your God.
net.DEU_20_19: If you besiege a city for a long time while attempting to capture it, you must not chop down its trees, for you may eat fruit from them and should not cut them down. A tree in the field is not human that you should besiege it!
net.DEU_20_20: However, you may chop down any tree you know is not suitable for food, and you may use it to build siege works against the city that is making war with you until that city falls.
net.DEU_21_1: If a homicide victim should be found lying in a field in the land the Lord your God is giving you, and no one knows who killed him,
net.DEU_21_2: your elders and judges must go out and measure how far it is to the cities in the vicinity of the corpse.
net.DEU_21_3: Then the elders of the city nearest to the corpse must take from the herd a heifer that has not been worked—that has never pulled with the yoke—
net.DEU_21_4: and bring the heifer down to a wadi with flowing water, to a valley that is neither plowed nor sown. There at the wadi they are to break the heifer’s neck.
net.DEU_21_5: Then the Levitical priests will approach (for the Lord your God has chosen them to serve him and to pronounce blessings in his name, and to decide every judicial verdict) ,
net.DEU_21_6: and all the elders of that city nearest the corpse must wash their hands over the heifer whose neck was broken in the valley.
net.DEU_21_7: Then they must proclaim, “Our hands have not spilled this blood, nor have we witnessed the crime.
net.DEU_21_8: Do not blame your people Israel whom you redeemed, O Lord, and do not hold them accountable for the bloodshed of an innocent person.” Then atonement will be made for the bloodshed.
net.DEU_21_9: In this manner you will purge the guilt of innocent blood from among you, for you must do what is right before the Lord. Laws Concerning Female Captives
net.DEU_21_10: When you go out to do battle with your enemies and the Lord your God allows you to prevail and you take prisoners,
net.DEU_21_11: if you should see among them an attractive woman whom you wish to take as a wife,
net.DEU_21_12: you may bring her back to your house. She must shave her head, trim her nails,
net.DEU_21_13: discard the clothing she was wearing when captured, and stay in your house, lamenting for her father and mother for a full month. After that you may sleep with her and become her husband and she your wife.
net.DEU_21_14: If you are not pleased with her, then you must let her go where she pleases. You cannot in any case sell her; you must not take advantage of her, since you have already humiliated her. Laws Concerning Children
net.DEU_21_15: Suppose a man has two wives, one whom he loves more than the other, and they both bear him sons, with the firstborn being the child of the less-loved wife.
net.DEU_21_16: In the day he divides his inheritance he must not appoint as firstborn the son of the favorite wife in place of the other wife’s son who is actually the firstborn.
net.DEU_21_17: Rather, he must acknowledge the son of the less-loved wife as firstborn and give him the double portion of all he has, for that son is the beginning of his father’s procreative power —to him should go the right of the firstborn.
net.DEU_21_18: If a person has a stubborn, rebellious son who pays no attention to his father or mother, and they discipline him to no avail,
net.DEU_21_19: his father and mother must seize him and bring him to the elders at the gate of his city.
net.DEU_21_20: They must declare to the elders of his city, “Our son is stubborn and rebellious and pays no attention to what we say—he is a glutton and drunkard.”
net.DEU_21_21: Then all the men of his city must stone him to death. In this way you will purge wickedness from among you, and all Israel will hear about it and be afraid. Disposition of a Criminal’s Remains
net.DEU_21_22: If a person commits a sin punishable by death and is executed, and you hang the corpse on a tree,
net.DEU_21_23: his body must not remain all night on the tree; instead you must make certain you bury him that same day, for the one who is left exposed on a tree is cursed by God. You must not defile your land that the Lord your God is giving you as an inheritance.
net.DEU_22_1: When you see your neighbor’s ox or sheep going astray, do not ignore it; you must return it without fail to your neighbor.
net.DEU_22_2: If the owner does not live near you or you do not know who the owner is, then you must corral the animal at your house and let it stay with you until the owner looks for it; then you must return it to him.
net.DEU_22_3: You shall do the same to his donkey, his clothes, or anything else your neighbor has lost and you have found; you must not refuse to get involved.
net.DEU_22_4: When you see your neighbor’s donkey or ox fallen along the road, do not ignore it; instead, you must be sure to help him get the animal on its feet again.
net.DEU_22_5: A woman must not wear men’s clothing, nor should a man dress up in women’s clothing, for anyone who does this is offensive to the Lord your God.
net.DEU_22_6: If you happen to notice a bird’s nest along the road, whether in a tree or on the ground, and there are chicks or eggs with the mother bird sitting on them, you must not take the mother from the young.
net.DEU_22_7: You must be sure to let the mother go, but you may take the young for yourself. Do this so that it may go well with you and you may have a long life.
net.DEU_22_8: If you build a new house, you must construct a guardrail around your roof to avoid being culpable in the event someone should fall from it. Illustrations of the Principle of Purity
net.DEU_22_9: You must not plant your vineyard with two kinds of seed; otherwise the entire yield, both of the seed you plant and the produce of the vineyard, will be defiled.
net.DEU_22_10: You must not plow with an ox and a donkey harnessed together.
net.DEU_22_11: You must not wear clothing made with wool and linen meshed together.
net.DEU_22_12: You shall make yourselves tassels for the four corners of the clothing you wear. Purity in the Marriage Relationship
net.DEU_22_13: Suppose a man marries a woman, sleeps with her, and then rejects her,
net.DEU_22_14: accusing her of impropriety and defaming her reputation by saying, “I married this woman but when I approached her for marital relations I discovered she was not a virgin!”
net.DEU_22_15: Then the father and mother of the young woman must produce the evidence of virginity for the elders of the city at the gate.
net.DEU_22_16: The young woman’s father must say to the elders, “I gave my daughter to this man and he has rejected her.
net.DEU_22_17: Moreover, he has raised accusations of impropriety by saying, ‘I discovered your daughter was not a virgin,’ but this is the evidence of my daughter’s virginity!” The cloth must then be spread out before the city’s elders.
net.DEU_22_18: The elders of that city must then seize the man and punish him.
net.DEU_22_19: They will fine him 100 shekels of silver and give them to the young woman’s father, for the man who made the accusation ruined the reputation of an Israelite virgin. She will then become his wife, and he may never divorce her as long as he lives.
net.DEU_22_20: But if the accusation is true and the young woman was not a virgin,
net.DEU_22_21: the men of her city must bring the young woman to the door of her father’s house and stone her to death, for she has done a disgraceful thing in Israel by behaving like a prostitute while living in her father’s house. In this way you will purge the evil from among you.
net.DEU_22_22: If a man is discovered in bed with a married woman, both the man lying in bed with the woman and the woman herself must die; in this way you will purge the evil from Israel.
net.DEU_22_23: If a virgin is engaged to a man and another man meets her in the city and goes to bed with her,
net.DEU_22_24: you must bring the two of them to the gate of that city and stone them to death, the young woman because she did not cry out though in the city and the man because he violated his neighbor’s fiancée; in this way you will purge evil from among you.
net.DEU_22_25: But if the man came across the engaged woman in the field and overpowered her and raped her, then only the rapist must die.
net.DEU_22_26: You must not do anything to the young woman—she has done nothing deserving of death. This case is the same as when someone attacks another person and murders him,
net.DEU_22_27: for the man met her in the field and the engaged woman cried out, but there was no one to rescue her.
net.DEU_22_28: Suppose a man comes across a virgin who is not engaged and takes hold of her and sleeps with her and they are discovered.
net.DEU_22_29: The man who has slept with her must pay her father fifty shekels of silver and she must become his wife. Because he has humiliated her, he may never divorce her as long as he lives.
net.DEU_22_30: (23:1) A man may not marry his father’s former wife and in this way dishonor his father.
net.DEU_23_1: A man with crushed or severed genitals may not enter the assembly of the Lord.
net.DEU_23_2: A person of illegitimate birth may not enter the assembly of the Lord; to the tenth generation no one related to him may do so.
net.DEU_23_3: No Ammonite or Moabite may enter the assembly of the Lord; to the tenth generation none of their descendants shall ever do so,
net.DEU_23_4: for they did not meet you with food and water on the way as you came from Egypt, and furthermore, they hired Balaam son of Beor of Pethor in Aram Naharaim to curse you.
net.DEU_23_5: But the Lord your God refused to listen to Balaam and changed the curse to a blessing, for the Lord your God loves you.
net.DEU_23_6: You must not seek peace and prosperity for them through all the ages to come.
net.DEU_23_7: You must not hate an Edomite, for he is your relative; you must not hate an Egyptian, for you lived as a foreigner in his land.
net.DEU_23_8: Children of the third generation born to them may enter the assembly of the Lord. Purity in Personal Hygiene
net.DEU_23_9: When you go out as an army against your enemies, guard yourselves against anything impure.
net.DEU_23_10: If there is someone among you who is impure because of some nocturnal emission, he must leave the camp; he may not reenter it immediately.
net.DEU_23_11: When evening arrives he must wash himself with water, and then at sunset he may reenter the camp.
net.DEU_23_12: You are to have a place outside the camp to serve as a latrine.
net.DEU_23_13: You must have a spade among your other equipment, and when you relieve yourself outside you must dig a hole with the spade and then turn and cover your excrement.
net.DEU_23_14: For the Lord your God walks about in the middle of your camp to deliver you and defeat your enemies for you. Therefore your camp should be holy, so that he does not see anything indecent among you and turn away from you. Purity in the Treatment of the Unprivileged
net.DEU_23_15: You must not return an escaped slave to his master when he has run away to you.
net.DEU_23_16: Indeed, he may live among you in any place he chooses, in whichever of your villages he prefers; you must not oppress him. Cultic Prostitution Banned
net.DEU_23_17: There must never be a sacred prostitute among the young women of Israel nor a sacred male prostitute among the young men of Israel.
net.DEU_23_18: You must never bring the pay of a female prostitute or the wage of a male prostitute into the temple of the Lord your God in fulfillment of any vow, for both of these are abhorrent to the Lord your God. Respect for Others’ Property
net.DEU_23_19: You must not charge interest on a loan to your fellow Israelite, whether on money, food, or anything else that has been loaned with interest.
net.DEU_23_20: You may lend with interest to a foreigner, but not to your fellow Israelite; if you keep this command the Lord your God will bless you in all you undertake in the land you are about to enter to possess.
net.DEU_23_21: When you make a vow to the Lord your God you must not delay in fulfilling it, for otherwise he will surely hold you accountable as a sinner.
net.DEU_23_22: If you refrain from making a vow, it will not be sinful.
net.DEU_23_23: Whatever you vow, you must be careful to do what you have promised, such as what you have vowed to the Lord your God as a freewill offering.
net.DEU_23_24: When you enter the vineyard of your neighbor you may eat as many grapes as you please, but you must not take away any in a container.
net.DEU_23_25: When you go into the ripe grain fields of your neighbor you may pluck off the kernels with your hand, but you must not use a sickle on your neighbor’s ripe grain.
net.DEU_24_1: If a man marries a woman and she does not please him because he has found something indecent in her, then he may draw up a divorce document, give it to her, and evict her from his house.
net.DEU_24_2: When she has left him she may go and become someone else’s wife.
net.DEU_24_3: If the second husband rejects her and then divorces her, gives her the papers, and evicts her from his house, or if the second husband who married her dies,
net.DEU_24_4: her first husband who divorced her is not permitted to remarry her after she has become ritually impure, for that is offensive to the Lord. You must not bring guilt on the land that the Lord your God is giving you as an inheritance.
net.DEU_24_5: When a man is newly married, he need not go into the army nor be obligated in any way; he must be free to stay at home for a full year and bring joy to the wife he has married.
net.DEU_24_6: One must not take either lower or upper millstones as security on a loan, for that is like taking a life itself as security.
net.DEU_24_7: If a man is found kidnapping a person from among his fellow Israelites, and regards him as mere property and sells him, that kidnapper must die. In this way you will purge the evil from among you. Respect for Human Dignity
net.DEU_24_8: Be careful during an outbreak of leprosy to follow precisely all that the Levitical priests instruct you; as I have commanded them, so you should do.
net.DEU_24_9: Remember what the Lord your God did to Miriam along the way after you left Egypt.
net.DEU_24_10: When you make any kind of loan to your neighbor, you may not go into his house to claim what he is offering as security.
net.DEU_24_11: You must stand outside and the person to whom you are making the loan will bring out to you what he is offering as security.
net.DEU_24_12: If the person is poor you may not use what he gives you as security for a covering.
net.DEU_24_13: You must by all means return to him at sunset the item he gave you as security so that he may sleep in his outer garment and bless you for it; it will be considered a just deed by the Lord your God.
net.DEU_24_14: You must not oppress a lowly and poor servant, whether one from among your fellow Israelites or from the resident foreigners who are living in your land and villages.
net.DEU_24_15: You must pay his wage that very day before the sun sets, for he is poor and his life depends on it. Otherwise he will cry out to the Lord against you, and you will be guilty of sin.
net.DEU_24_16: Fathers must not be put to death for what their children do, nor children for what their fathers do; each must be put to death for his own sin.
net.DEU_24_17: You must not pervert justice due a resident foreigner or an orphan, or take a widow’s garment as security for a loan.
net.DEU_24_18: Remember that you were slaves in Egypt and that the Lord your God redeemed you from there; therefore I am commanding you to do all this.
net.DEU_24_19: Whenever you reap your harvest in your field and leave some unraked grain there, you must not return to get it; it should go to the resident foreigner, orphan, and widow so that the Lord your God may bless all the work you do.
net.DEU_24_20: When you beat your olive tree you must not repeat the procedure; the remaining olives belong to the resident foreigner, orphan, and widow.
net.DEU_24_21: When you gather the grapes of your vineyard you must not do so a second time; they should go to the resident foreigner, orphan, and widow.
net.DEU_24_22: Remember that you were slaves in the land of Egypt; therefore, I am commanding you to do all this.
net.DEU_25_1: If controversy arises between people, they should go to court for judgment. When the judges hear the case, they shall exonerate the innocent but condemn the guilty.
net.DEU_25_2: Then, if the guilty person is sentenced to a beating, the judge shall force him to lie down and be beaten in his presence with the number of blows his wicked behavior deserves.
net.DEU_25_3: The judge may sentence him to forty blows, but no more. If he is struck with more than these, you might view your fellow Israelite with contempt.
net.DEU_25_4: You must not muzzle your ox when it is treading grain. Respect for the Sanctity of Others
net.DEU_25_5: If brothers live together and one of them dies without having a son, the dead man’s wife must not remarry someone outside the family. Instead, her late husband’s brother must go to her, marry her, and perform the duty of a brother-in-law.
net.DEU_25_6: Then the first son she bears will continue the name of the dead brother, thus preventing his name from being blotted out of Israel.
net.DEU_25_7: But if the man does not want to marry his brother’s widow, then she must go to the elders at the town gate and say, “My husband’s brother refuses to preserve his brother’s name in Israel; he is unwilling to perform the duty of a brother-in-law to me!”
net.DEU_25_8: Then the elders of his city must summon him and speak to him. If he persists, saying, “I don’t want to marry her,”
net.DEU_25_9: then his sister-in-law must approach him in view of the elders, remove his sandal from his foot, and spit in his face. She will then respond, “Thus may it be done to any man who does not maintain his brother’s family line!”
net.DEU_25_10: His family name will be referred to in Israel as “the family of the one whose sandal was removed.”
net.DEU_25_11: If two men get into a hand-to-hand fight, and the wife of one of them gets involved to help her husband against his attacker, and she reaches out her hand and grabs his private parts,
net.DEU_25_12: then you must cut off her hand—do not pity her.
net.DEU_25_13: You must not have in your bag different stone weights, a heavy and a light one.
net.DEU_25_14: You must not have in your house different measuring containers, a large and a small one.
net.DEU_25_15: You must have an accurate and correct stone weight and an accurate and correct measuring container, so that your life may be extended in the land the Lord your God is about to give you.
net.DEU_25_16: For anyone who acts dishonestly in these ways is abhorrent to the Lord your God. Treatment of the Amalekites
net.DEU_25_17: Remember what the Amalekites did to you on your way from Egypt,
net.DEU_25_18: how they met you along the way and cut off all your stragglers in the rear of the march when you were exhausted and tired; they were unafraid of God.
net.DEU_25_19: So when the Lord your God gives you relief from all the enemies who surround you in the land he is giving you as an inheritance, you must wipe out the memory of the Amalekites from under heaven —do not forget!
net.DEU_26_1: When you enter the land that the Lord your God is giving you as an inheritance, and you occupy it and live in it,
net.DEU_26_2: you must take the first of all the ground’s produce you harvest from the land the Lord your God is giving you, place it in a basket, and go to the place where he chooses to locate his name.
net.DEU_26_3: You must go to the priest in office at that time and say to him, “I declare today to the Lord your God that I have come into the land that the Lord promised to our ancestors to give us.”
net.DEU_26_4: The priest will then take the basket from you and set it before the altar of the Lord your God.
net.DEU_26_5: Then you must affirm before the Lord your God, “A wandering Aramean was my ancestor, and he went down to Egypt and lived there as a foreigner with a household few in number, but there he became a great, powerful, and numerous people.
net.DEU_26_6: But the Egyptians mistreated and oppressed us, forcing us to do burdensome labor.
net.DEU_26_7: So we cried out to the Lord, the God of our ancestors, and he heard us and saw our humiliation, toil, and oppression.
net.DEU_26_8: Therefore the Lord brought us out of Egypt with tremendous strength and power, as well as with great awe-inspiring signs and wonders.
net.DEU_26_9: Then he brought us to this place and gave us this land, a land flowing with milk and honey.
net.DEU_26_10: So now, look! I have brought the first of the ground’s produce that you, Lord, have given me.” Then you must set it down before the Lord your God and worship before him.
net.DEU_26_11: You will celebrate all the good things that the Lord your God has given you and your family, along with the Levites and the resident foreigners among you. Presentation of the Third-year Tithe
net.DEU_26_12: When you finish tithing all your income in the third year (the year of tithing), you must give it to the Levites, the resident foreigners, the orphans, and the widows so that they may eat to their satisfaction in your villages.
net.DEU_26_13: Then you shall say before the Lord your God, “I have removed the sacred offering from my house and given it to the Levites, the resident foreigners, the orphans, and the widows just as you have commanded me. I have not violated or forgotten your commandments.
net.DEU_26_14: I have not eaten anything when I was in mourning, or removed any of it while ceremonially unclean, or offered any of it to the dead; I have obeyed you and have done everything you have commanded me.
net.DEU_26_15: Look down from your holy dwelling place in heaven and bless your people Israel and the land you have given us, just as you promised our ancestors—a land flowing with milk and honey.” Narrative Interlude
net.DEU_26_16: Today the Lord your God is commanding you to keep these statutes and ordinances, something you must do with all your heart and soul.
net.DEU_26_17: Today you have declared the Lord to be your God, and that you will walk in his ways, keep his statutes, commandments, and ordinances, and obey him.
net.DEU_26_18: And today the Lord has declared you to be his special people (as he already promised you) so you may keep all his commandments.
net.DEU_26_19: Then he will elevate you above all the nations he has made and you will receive praise, fame, and honor. You will be a people holy to the Lord your God, as he has said.
net.DEU_27_1: Then Moses and the elders of Israel commanded the people: “Pay attention to all the commandments I am giving you today.
net.DEU_27_2: When you cross the Jordan River to the land the Lord your God is giving you, you must erect great stones and cover them with plaster.
net.DEU_27_3: Then you must inscribe on them all the words of this law when you cross over, so that you may enter the land the Lord your God is giving you, a land flowing with milk and honey just as the Lord, the God of your ancestors, said to you.
net.DEU_27_4: So when you cross the Jordan you must erect on Mount Ebal these stones about which I am commanding you today, and you must cover them with plaster.
net.DEU_27_5: Then you must build an altar there to the Lord your God, an altar of stones—do not use an iron tool on them.
net.DEU_27_6: You must build the altar of the Lord your God with whole stones and offer burnt offerings on it to the Lord your God.
net.DEU_27_7: Also you must offer fellowship offerings and eat them there, rejoicing before the Lord your God.
net.DEU_27_8: You must inscribe on the stones all the words of this law, making them clear.”
net.DEU_27_9: Then Moses and the Levitical priests spoke to all Israel: “Be quiet and pay attention, Israel. Today you have become the people of the Lord your God.
net.DEU_27_10: You must obey him and keep his commandments and statutes that I am giving you today.”
net.DEU_27_11: Moreover, Moses commanded the people that day:
net.DEU_27_12: “The following tribes must stand to bless the people on Mount Gerizim when you cross the Jordan: Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Joseph, and Benjamin.
net.DEU_27_13: And these other tribes must stand for the curse on Mount Ebal: Reuben, Gad, Asher, Zebulun, Dan, and Naphtali. The Covenant Curses
net.DEU_27_14: “The Levites will call out to every Israelite with a loud voice:
net.DEU_27_15: ‘Cursed is the one who makes a carved or metal image—something abhorrent to the Lord, the work of the craftsman —and sets it up in a secret place.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_27_16: ‘Cursed is the one who disrespects his father and mother.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_27_17: ‘Cursed is the one who moves his neighbor’s boundary marker.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_27_18: ‘Cursed is the one who misleads a blind person on the road.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_27_19: ‘Cursed is the one who perverts justice for the resident foreigner, the orphan, and the widow.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_27_20: ‘Cursed is the one who goes to bed with his father’s former wife, for he dishonors his father.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_27_21: ‘Cursed is the one who commits bestiality.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_27_22: ‘Cursed is the one who goes to bed with his sister, the daughter of either his father or mother.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_27_23: ‘Cursed is the one who goes to bed with his mother-in-law.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_27_24: ‘Cursed is the one who kills his neighbor in private.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_27_25: ‘Cursed is the one who takes a bribe to kill an innocent person.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_27_26: ‘Cursed is the one who refuses to keep the words of this law.’ Then all the people will say, ‘Amen!’
net.DEU_28_1: “If you indeed obey the Lord your God and are careful to observe all his commandments I am giving you today, the Lord your God will elevate you above all the nations of the earth.
net.DEU_28_2: All these blessings will come to you in abundance if you obey the Lord your God:
net.DEU_28_3: You will be blessed in the city and blessed in the field.
net.DEU_28_4: Your children will be blessed, as well as the produce of your soil, the offspring of your livestock, the calves of your herds, and the lambs of your flocks.
net.DEU_28_5: Your basket and your mixing bowl will be blessed.
net.DEU_28_6: You will be blessed when you come in and blessed when you go out.
net.DEU_28_7: The Lord will cause your enemies who attack you to be struck down before you; they will attack you from one direction but flee from you in seven different directions.
net.DEU_28_8: The Lord will decree blessing for you with respect to your barns and in everything you do—yes, he will bless you in the land he is giving you.
net.DEU_28_9: The Lord will designate you as his holy people just as he promised you, if you keep his commandments and obey him.
net.DEU_28_10: Then all the peoples of the earth will see that you belong to the Lord, and they will respect you.
net.DEU_28_11: The Lord will greatly multiply your children, the offspring of your livestock, and the produce of your soil in the land that he promised your ancestors he would give you.
net.DEU_28_12: The Lord will open for you his good treasure house, the heavens, to give you rain for the land in its season and to bless all you do; you will lend to many nations but you will not borrow from any.
net.DEU_28_13: The Lord will make you the head and not the tail, and you will always end up at the top and not at the bottom, if you obey his commandments that I am urging you today to be careful to do.
net.DEU_28_14: But you must not turn away from all the commandments I am giving you today, to either the right or left, nor pursue other gods and worship them. Curses as Reversal of Blessings
net.DEU_28_15: “But if you ignore the Lord your God and are not careful to keep all his commandments and statutes I am giving you today, then all these curses will come upon you in full force:
net.DEU_28_16: You will be cursed in the city and cursed in the field.
net.DEU_28_17: Your basket and your mixing bowl will be cursed.
net.DEU_28_18: Your children will be cursed, as well as the produce of your soil, the calves of your herds, and the lambs of your flocks.
net.DEU_28_19: You will be cursed when you come in and cursed when you go out. Curses by Disease and Drought
net.DEU_28_20: “The Lord will send on you a curse, confusing you and opposing you in everything you undertake until you are destroyed and quickly perish because of the evil of your deeds, in that you have forsaken me.
net.DEU_28_21: The Lord will plague you with deadly diseases until he has completely removed you from the land you are about to possess.
net.DEU_28_22: He will afflict you with weakness, fever, inflammation, infection, sword, blight, and mildew; these will attack you until you perish.
net.DEU_28_23: The sky above your heads will be bronze and the earth beneath you iron.
net.DEU_28_24: The Lord will make the rain of your land powder and dust; it will come down on you from the sky until you are destroyed. Curses by Defeat and Deportation
net.DEU_28_25: “The Lord will allow you to be struck down before your enemies; you will attack them from one direction but flee from them in seven directions and will become an object of terror to all the kingdoms of the earth.
net.DEU_28_26: Your carcasses will be food for every bird of the sky and wild animal of the earth, and there will be no one to chase them off.
net.DEU_28_27: The Lord will afflict you with the boils of Egypt and with tumors, eczema, and scabies, all of which cannot be healed.
net.DEU_28_28: The Lord will also subject you to madness, blindness, and confusion of mind.
net.DEU_28_29: You will feel your way along at noon like the blind person does in darkness and you will not succeed in anything you do; you will be constantly oppressed and continually robbed, with no one to save you.
net.DEU_28_30: You will be engaged to a woman, and another man will rape her. You will build a house but not live in it. You will plant a vineyard but not even begin to use it.
net.DEU_28_31: Your ox will be slaughtered before your very eyes, but you will not eat of it. Your donkey will be stolen from you as you watch and will not be returned to you. Your flock of sheep will be given to your enemies, and there will be no one to save you.
net.DEU_28_32: Your sons and daughters will be given to another people while you look on in vain all day, and you will be powerless to do anything about it.
net.DEU_28_33: As for the produce of your land and all your labor, a people you do not know will consume it, and you will be nothing but oppressed and crushed for the rest of your lives.
net.DEU_28_34: You will go insane from seeing all this.
net.DEU_28_35: The Lord will afflict you in your knees and on your legs with painful, incurable boils—from the soles of your feet to the top of your head.
net.DEU_28_36: The Lord will force you and your king whom you will appoint over you to go away to a people whom you and your ancestors have not known, and you will serve other gods of wood and stone there.
net.DEU_28_37: You will become an occasion of horror, a proverb, and an object of ridicule to all the peoples to whom the Lord will drive you. The Curse of Reversed Status
net.DEU_28_38: “You will take much seed to the field but gather little harvest, because locusts will consume it.
net.DEU_28_39: You will plant vineyards and cultivate them, but you will not drink wine or gather in grapes, because worms will eat them.
net.DEU_28_40: You will have olive trees throughout your territory, but you will not anoint yourself with olive oil, because the olives will drop off the trees while still unripe.
net.DEU_28_41: You will bear sons and daughters but not keep them, because they will be taken into captivity.
net.DEU_28_42: Whirring locusts will take over every tree and all the produce of your soil.
net.DEU_28_43: The resident foreigners who reside among you will become higher and higher over you, and you will become lower and lower.
net.DEU_28_44: They will lend to you, but you will not lend to them; they will become the head, and you will become the tail!
net.DEU_28_45: “All these curses will fall on you, pursuing and overtaking you until you are destroyed, because you would not obey the Lord your God by keeping his commandments and statutes that he has given you.
net.DEU_28_46: These curses will be a perpetual sign and wonder with reference to you and your descendants. The Curse of Military Siege
net.DEU_28_47: “Because you have not served the Lord your God joyfully and wholeheartedly with the abundance of everything you have,
net.DEU_28_48: instead in hunger, thirst, nakedness, and poverty you will serve your enemies whom the Lord will send against you. They will place an iron yoke on your neck until they have destroyed you.
net.DEU_28_49: The Lord will raise up a distant nation against you, one from the other side of the earth as the eagle flies, a nation whose language you will not understand,
net.DEU_28_50: a nation of stern appearance that will have no regard for the elderly or pity for the young.
net.DEU_28_51: They will devour the offspring of your livestock and the produce of your soil until you are destroyed. They will not leave you with any grain, new wine, olive oil, calves of your herds, or lambs of your flocks until they have destroyed you.
net.DEU_28_52: They will besiege all of your villages until all of your high and fortified walls collapse—those in which you put your confidence throughout the land. They will besiege all your villages throughout the land the Lord your God has given you.
net.DEU_28_53: You will then eat your own offspring, the flesh of the sons and daughters the Lord your God has given you, because of the severity of the siege by which your enemies will constrict you.
net.DEU_28_54: The man among you who is by nature tender and sensitive will turn against his brother, his beloved wife, and his remaining children.
net.DEU_28_55: He will withhold from all of them his children’s flesh that he is eating (since there is nothing else left), because of the severity of the siege by which your enemy will constrict you in your villages.
net.DEU_28_56: Likewise, the most tender and delicate of your women, who would never think of putting even the sole of her foot on the ground because of her daintiness, will turn against her beloved husband, her sons and daughters,
net.DEU_28_57: and will secretly eat her afterbirth and her newborn children (since she has nothing else), because of the severity of the siege by which your enemy will constrict you in your villages. The Curse of Covenant Termination
net.DEU_28_58: “If you refuse to obey all the words of this law, the things written in this scroll, and refuse to fear this glorious and awesome name, the Lord your God,
net.DEU_28_59: then the Lord will increase your punishments and those of your descendants—great and long-lasting afflictions and severe, enduring illnesses.
net.DEU_28_60: He will infect you with all the diseases of Egypt that you dreaded, and they will persistently afflict you.
net.DEU_28_61: Moreover, the Lord will bring upon you every kind of sickness and plague not mentioned in this scroll of commandments, until you have perished.
net.DEU_28_62: There will be very few of you left, though at one time you were as numerous as the stars in the sky, because you will have disobeyed the Lord your God.
net.DEU_28_63: This is what will happen: Just as the Lord delighted to do good for you and make you numerous, so he will also take delight in destroying and decimating you. You will be uprooted from the land you are about to possess.
net.DEU_28_64: The Lord will scatter you among all nations, from one end of the earth to the other. There you will worship other gods that neither you nor your ancestors have known, gods of wood and stone.
net.DEU_28_65: Among those nations you will have no rest, nor will there be a place of peaceful rest for the soles of your feet, for there the Lord will give you an anxious heart, failing eyesight, and a spirit of despair.
net.DEU_28_66: Your life will hang in doubt before you; you will be terrified by night and day and will have no certainty of surviving from one day to the next.
net.DEU_28_67: In the morning you will say, ‘If only it were evening!’ And in the evening you will say, ‘I wish it were morning!’ because of the things you will fear and the things you will see.
net.DEU_28_68: Then the Lord will make you return to Egypt by ship, over a route I said to you that you would never see again. There you will sell yourselves to your enemies as male and female slaves, but no one will buy you.”
net.DEU_29_1: (28:69) These are the words of the covenant that the Lord commanded Moses to make with the people of Israel in the land of Moab, in addition to the covenant he had made with them at Horeb. The Exodus, Wandering, and Conquest Reviewed
net.DEU_29_2: Moses proclaimed to all Israel as follows: “You have seen all that the Lord did in the land of Egypt to Pharaoh, all his servants, and his land.
net.DEU_29_3: Your eyes have seen the great judgments, those signs and mighty wonders.
net.DEU_29_4: But to this very day the Lord has not given you an understanding mind, perceptive eyes, or discerning ears!
net.DEU_29_5: I have led you through the wilderness for forty years. Your clothing has not worn out nor have your sandals deteriorated.
net.DEU_29_6: You have eaten no bread and drunk no wine or beer—all so that you might know that I am the Lord your God!
net.DEU_29_7: When you came to this place King Sihon of Heshbon and King Og of Bashan came out to make war and we defeated them.
net.DEU_29_8: Then we took their land and gave it as an inheritance to Reuben, Gad, and half the tribe of Manasseh. The Present Covenant Setting
net.DEU_29_9: “Therefore, keep the terms of this covenant and obey them so that you may be successful in everything you do.
net.DEU_29_10: You are standing today, all of you, before the Lord your God—the heads of your tribes, your elders, your officials, every Israelite man,
net.DEU_29_11: your infants, your wives, and the resident foreigners living in your encampment, those who chop wood and those who carry water—
net.DEU_29_12: so that you may enter by oath into the covenant the Lord your God is making with you today.
net.DEU_29_13: Today he will affirm that you are his people and that he is your God, just as he promised you and as he swore by oath to your ancestors Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
net.DEU_29_14: It is not with you alone that I am making this covenant by oath,
net.DEU_29_15: but with whoever stands with us here today before the Lord our God as well as those not with us here today. The Results of Disobedience
net.DEU_29_16: “(For you know how we lived in the land of Egypt and how we crossed through the nations as we traveled.
net.DEU_29_17: You have seen their detestable things and idols of wood, stone, silver, and gold.)
net.DEU_29_18: Beware that the heart of no man, woman, clan, or tribe among you turns away from the Lord our God today to pursue and serve the gods of those nations; beware that there is among you no root producing poisonous and bitter fruit.
net.DEU_29_19: When such a person hears the words of this oath he secretly blesses himself and says, ‘I will have peace though I continue to walk with a stubborn spirit.’ This will destroy the watered ground with the parched.
net.DEU_29_20: The Lord will be unwilling to forgive him, and his intense anger will rage against that man; all the curses written in this scroll will fall upon him, and the Lord will obliterate his name from memory.
net.DEU_29_21: The Lord will single him out for judgment from all the tribes of Israel according to all the curses of the covenant written in this scroll of the law.
net.DEU_29_22: The generation to come—your descendants who will rise up after you, as well as the foreigner who will come from distant places—will see the afflictions of that land and the illnesses that the Lord has brought on it.
net.DEU_29_23: The whole land will be covered with brimstone, salt, and burning debris; it will not be planted nor will it sprout or produce grass. It will resemble the destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboyim, which the Lord destroyed in his intense anger.
net.DEU_29_24: Then all the nations will ask, ‘Why has the Lord done all this to this land? What is this fierce, heated display of anger all about?’
net.DEU_29_25: Then people will say, ‘Because they abandoned the covenant of the Lord, the God of their ancestors, which he made with them when he brought them out of the land of Egypt.
net.DEU_29_26: They went and served other gods and worshiped them, gods they did not know and that he did not permit them to worship.
net.DEU_29_27: That is why the Lord’s anger erupted against this land, bringing on it all the curses written in this scroll.
net.DEU_29_28: So the Lord has uprooted them from their land in anger, wrath, and great rage and has deported them to another land, as is clear today.’
net.DEU_29_29: The secret things belong to the Lord our God, but those that are revealed belong to us and our descendants forever, so that we might obey all the words of this law.
net.DEU_30_1: “When you have experienced all these things, both the blessings and the curses I have set before you, you will reflect upon them in all the nations where the Lord your God has banished you.
net.DEU_30_2: Then if you and your descendants turn to the Lord your God and obey him with your whole mind and being just as I am commanding you today,
net.DEU_30_3: the Lord your God will reverse your captivity and have pity on you. He will turn and gather you from all the peoples among whom he has scattered you.
net.DEU_30_4: Even if your exiles are in the most distant land, from there the Lord your God will gather you and bring you back.
net.DEU_30_5: Then he will bring you to the land your ancestors possessed and you also will possess it; he will do better for you and multiply you more than he did your ancestors.
net.DEU_30_6: The Lord your God will also cleanse your heart, and the hearts of your descendants so that you may love him with all your mind and being and so that you may live.
net.DEU_30_7: Then the Lord your God will put all these curses on your enemies, on those who hate you and persecute you.
net.DEU_30_8: You will return and obey the Lord, keeping all his commandments I am giving you today.
net.DEU_30_9: The Lord your God will make the labor of your hands abundantly successful and multiply your children, the offspring of your cattle, and the produce of your soil. For the Lord will once more rejoice over you to make you prosperous just as he rejoiced over your ancestors,
net.DEU_30_10: if you obey the Lord your God and keep his commandments and statutes that are written in this scroll of the law. But you must turn to him with your whole mind and being. Exhortation to Covenant Obedience
net.DEU_30_11: “This commandment I am giving you today is not too difficult for you, nor is it too remote.
net.DEU_30_12: It is not in heaven, as though one must say, ‘Who will go up to heaven to get it for us and proclaim it to us so we may obey it?’
net.DEU_30_13: And it is not across the sea, as though one must say, ‘Who will cross over to the other side of the sea and get it for us and proclaim it to us so we may obey it?’
net.DEU_30_14: For the thing is very near you—it is in your mouth and in your mind so that you can do it.
net.DEU_30_15: “Look! I have set before you today life and prosperity on the one hand, and death and disaster on the other.
net.DEU_30_16: What I am commanding you today is to love the Lord your God, to walk in his ways, and to obey his commandments, his statutes, and his ordinances. Then you will live and become numerous and the Lord your God will bless you in the land that you are about to possess.
net.DEU_30_17: However, if you turn aside and do not obey, but are lured away to worship and serve other gods,
net.DEU_30_18: I declare to you this very day that you will certainly perish! You will not extend your time in the land you are crossing the Jordan to possess.
net.DEU_30_19: Today I invoke heaven and earth as witnesses against you that I have set life and death, blessing and curse, before you. Therefore choose life so that you and your descendants may live!
net.DEU_30_20: I also call on you to love the Lord your God, to obey him and be loyal to him, for he gives you life and enables you to live continually in the land the Lord promised to give to your ancestors Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.”
net.DEU_31_1: Then Moses went and spoke these words to all Israel.
net.DEU_31_2: He said to them, “Today I am 120 years old. I am no longer able to get about, and the Lord has said to me, ‘You will not cross the Jordan.’
net.DEU_31_3: As for the Lord your God, he is about to cross over before you; he will destroy these nations before you, and you will dispossess them. As for Joshua, he is about to cross before you just as the Lord has said.
net.DEU_31_4: The Lord will do to them just what he did to Sihon and Og, the Amorite kings, and to their land, which he destroyed.
net.DEU_31_5: The Lord will deliver them over to you, and you will do to them according to the whole commandment I have given you.
net.DEU_31_6: Be strong and courageous! Do not fear or tremble before them, for the Lord your God is the one who is going with you. He will not fail you or abandon you!”
net.DEU_31_7: Then Moses called out to Joshua in the presence of all Israel, “Be strong and courageous, for you will accompany these people to the land that the Lord promised to give their ancestors, and you will enable them to inherit it.
net.DEU_31_8: The Lord is indeed going before you—he will be with you; he will not fail you or abandon you. Do not be afraid or discouraged!” The Deposit of the Covenant Text
net.DEU_31_9: Then Moses wrote down this law and gave it to the Levitical priests, who carry the ark of the Lord’s covenant, and to all Israel’s elders.
net.DEU_31_10: He commanded them: “At the end of seven years, at the appointed time of the cancellation of debts, at the Feast of Shelters,
net.DEU_31_11: when all Israel comes to appear before the Lord your God in the place he chooses, you must read this law before them within their hearing.
net.DEU_31_12: Gather the people—men, women, and children, as well as the resident foreigners in your villages—so they may hear and thus learn about and fear the Lord your God and carefully obey all the words of this law.
net.DEU_31_13: Then their children, who have not known this law, will also hear about and learn to fear the Lord your God for as long as you live in the land you are crossing the Jordan to possess.” The Commissioning of Joshua
net.DEU_31_14: Then the Lord said to Moses, “The day of your death is near. Summon Joshua and present yourselves in the tent of meeting so that I can commission him.” So Moses and Joshua presented themselves in the tent of meeting.
net.DEU_31_15: The Lord appeared in the tent in a pillar of cloud that stood above the door of the tent.
net.DEU_31_16: Then the Lord said to Moses, “You are about to die, and then these people will begin to prostitute themselves with the foreign gods of the land into which they are going. They will reject me and break my covenant that I have made with them.
net.DEU_31_17: At that time my anger will erupt against them, and I will abandon them and hide my face from them until they are devoured. Many disasters and distresses will overcome them so that they will say at that time, ‘Have not these disasters overcome us because our God is not among us ?’
net.DEU_31_18: But I will certainly hide myself at that time because of all the wickedness they will have done by turning to other gods.
net.DEU_31_19: Now write down for yourselves the following song and teach it to the Israelites. Put it into their very mouths so that this song may serve as my witness against the Israelites!
net.DEU_31_20: For after I have brought them to the land I promised to their ancestors—one flowing with milk and honey—and they eat their fill and become fat, then they will turn to other gods and worship them; they will reject me and break my covenant.
net.DEU_31_21: Then when many disasters and distresses overcome them this song will testify against them, for their descendants will not forget it. I know the intentions they have in mind today, even before I bring them to the land I have promised.”
net.DEU_31_22: So on that day Moses wrote down this song and taught it to the Israelites,
net.DEU_31_23: and the Lord commissioned Joshua son of Nun, “Be strong and courageous, for you will take the Israelites to the land I have promised them, and I will be with you.” Anticipation of Disobedience
net.DEU_31_24: When Moses finished writing on a scroll the words of this law in their entirety,
net.DEU_31_25: he commanded the Levites who carried the ark of the Lord’s covenant,
net.DEU_31_26: “Take this scroll of the law and place it beside the ark of the covenant of the Lord your God. It will remain there as a witness against you,
net.DEU_31_27: for I know about your rebellion and stubbornness. Indeed, even while I have been living among you to this very day, you have rebelled against the Lord; you will be even more rebellious after my death!
net.DEU_31_28: Gather to me all your tribal elders and officials so I can speak to them directly about these things and call the heavens and the earth to witness against them.
net.DEU_31_29: For I know that after I die you will totally corrupt yourselves and turn away from the path I have commanded you to walk. Disaster will confront you in future days because you will act wickedly before the Lord, inciting him to anger because of your actions.”
net.DEU_31_30: Then Moses recited the words of this song from start to finish in the hearing of the whole assembly of Israel:
net.DEU_32_1: Listen, O heavens, and I will speak; hear, O earth, the words of my mouth.
net.DEU_32_2: My teaching will drop like the rain, my sayings will drip like the dew, as rain drops upon the grass, and showers upon new growth.
net.DEU_32_3: For I will proclaim the name of the Lord; you must acknowledge the greatness of our God.
net.DEU_32_4: As for the Rock, his work is perfect, for all his ways are just. He is a reliable God who is never unjust, he is fair and upright.
net.DEU_32_5: His people have been unfaithful to him; they have not acted like his children —this is their sin. They are a perverse and deceitful generation.
net.DEU_32_6: Is this how you repay the Lord, you foolish, unwise people? Is he not your father, your Creator? He has made you and established you.
net.DEU_32_7: Remember the ancient days; bear in mind the years of past generations. Ask your father and he will inform you, your elders, and they will tell you.
net.DEU_32_8: When the Most High gave the nations their inheritance, when he divided up humankind, he set the boundaries of the peoples, according to the number of the heavenly assembly.
net.DEU_32_9: For the Lord’s allotment is his people, Jacob is his special possession.
net.DEU_32_10: The Lord found him in a desolate land, in an empty wasteland where animals howl. He continually guarded him and taught him; he continually protected him like the pupil of his eye.
net.DEU_32_11: Like an eagle that stirs up its nest, that hovers over its young, so the Lord spread out his wings and took him, he lifted him up on his pinions.
net.DEU_32_12: The Lord alone was guiding him, no foreign god was with him.
net.DEU_32_13: He enabled him to travel over the high terrain of the land, and he ate of the produce of the fields. He provided honey for him from the cliffs, and olive oil from the hardest of rocks,
net.DEU_32_14: butter from the herd and milk from the flock, along with the fat of lambs, rams and goats of Bashan, along with the best of the kernels of wheat; and from the juice of grapes you drank wine. Israel’s Rebellion
net.DEU_32_15: But Jeshurun became fat and kicked; you got fat, thick, and stuffed! Then he deserted the God who made him, and treated the Rock who saved him with contempt.
net.DEU_32_16: They made him jealous with other gods, they enraged him with abhorrent idols.
net.DEU_32_17: They sacrificed to demons, not God, to gods they had not known; to new gods who had recently come along, gods your ancestors had not known about.
net.DEU_32_18: You forgot the Rock who fathered you, and put out of mind the God who gave you birth. A Word of Judgment
net.DEU_32_19: But the Lord took note and despised them because his sons and daughters enraged him.
net.DEU_32_20: He said, “I will reject them. I will see what will happen to them; for they are a perverse generation, children who show no loyalty.
net.DEU_32_21: They have made me jealous with false gods, enraging me with their worthless gods; so I will make them jealous with a people they do not recognize, with a nation slow to learn I will enrage them.
net.DEU_32_22: For a fire has been kindled by my anger, and it burns to lowest Sheol; it consumes the earth and its produce, and ignites the foundations of the mountains.
net.DEU_32_23: I will increase their disasters; I will use up my arrows on them.
net.DEU_32_24: They will be starved by famine, eaten by plague, and bitterly stung; I will send the teeth of wild animals against them, along with the poison of creatures that crawl in the dust.
net.DEU_32_25: The sword will make people childless outside, and terror will do so inside; they will destroy both the young man and the virgin, the infant and the gray-haired man. The Weakness of Other Gods
net.DEU_32_26: “I said, ‘I want to cut them in pieces. I want to make people forget they ever existed.
net.DEU_32_27: But I fear the reaction of their enemies, for their adversaries would misunderstand and say, “Our power is great, and the Lord has not done all this!”’
net.DEU_32_28: They are a nation devoid of wisdom, and there is no understanding among them.
net.DEU_32_29: I wish that they were wise and could understand this, and that they could comprehend what will happen to them.”
net.DEU_32_30: How can one man chase a thousand of them, and two pursue ten thousand, unless their Rock had delivered them up — and the Lord had handed them over?
net.DEU_32_31: For our enemies’ rock is not like our Rock, as even our enemies concede.
net.DEU_32_32: For their vine is from the stock of Sodom, and from the fields of Gomorrah. Their grapes contain venom; their clusters of grapes are bitter.
net.DEU_32_33: Their wine is snakes’ poison, the deadly venom of cobras.
net.DEU_32_34: “Is this not stored up with me?” says the Lord, “Is it not sealed up in my storehouses?
net.DEU_32_35: I will get revenge and pay them back at the time their foot slips; for the day of their disaster is near, and the impending judgment is rushing upon them!”
net.DEU_32_36: The Lord will judge his people, and will change his plans concerning his servants; when he sees that their power has disappeared, and that no one is left, whether confined or set free.
net.DEU_32_37: He will say, “Where are their gods, the rock in whom they sought security,
net.DEU_32_38: who ate the best of their sacrifices, and drank the wine of their drink offerings? Let them rise and help you; let them be your refuge! The Vindication of the Lord
net.DEU_32_39: “See now that I, indeed I, am he!” says the Lord, “and there is no other god besides me. I kill and give life, I smash and I heal, and none can resist my power.
net.DEU_32_40: For I raise up my hand to heaven, and say, ‘As surely as I live forever,
net.DEU_32_41: I will sharpen my lightning-like sword, and my hand will grasp hold of the weapon of judgment; I will execute vengeance on my foes, and repay those who hate me!
net.DEU_32_42: I will make my arrows drunk with blood, and my sword will devour flesh— the blood of the slaughtered and captured, the chief of the enemy’s leaders.’”
net.DEU_32_43: Cry out, O nations, with his people, for he will avenge his servants’ blood; he will take vengeance against his enemies, and make atonement for his land and people. Narrative Interlude
net.DEU_32_44: Then Moses went with Joshua son of Nun and recited all the words of this song to the people.
net.DEU_32_45: When Moses finished reciting all these words to all Israel
net.DEU_32_46: he said to them, “Keep in mind all the words I am solemnly proclaiming to you today; you must command your children to observe carefully all the words of this law.
net.DEU_32_47: For this is no idle word for you—it is your life! By this word you will live a long time in the land you are about to cross the Jordan to possess.” Instructions about Moses’ Death
net.DEU_32_48: Then the Lord said to Moses that same day,
net.DEU_32_49: “Go up to this Abarim hill country, to Mount Nebo (which is in the land of Moab opposite Jericho), and look at the land of Canaan that I am giving to the Israelites as a possession.
net.DEU_32_50: You will die on the mountain that you ascend and join your deceased ancestors, just as Aaron your brother died on Mount Hor and joined his deceased ancestors,
net.DEU_32_51: for both of you rebelled against me among the Israelites at the waters of Meribah Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin when you did not show me proper respect among the Israelites.
net.DEU_32_52: You will see the land before you, but you will not enter the land that I am giving to the Israelites.”
net.DEU_33_1: This is the blessing Moses the man of God pronounced upon the Israelites before his death.
net.DEU_33_2: He said: A Historical Review “The Lord came from Sinai and revealed himself to Israel from Seir. He appeared in splendor from Mount Paran, and came forth with ten thousand holy ones. With his right hand he gave a fiery law to them.
net.DEU_33_3: Surely he loves the people; all your holy ones are in your power. And they sit at your feet, each receiving your words.
net.DEU_33_4: Moses delivered to us a law, an inheritance for the assembly of Jacob.
net.DEU_33_5: The Lord was king over Jeshurun, when the leaders of the people assembled, the tribes of Israel together. Blessing on Reuben
net.DEU_33_6: “May Reuben live and not die, and may his people multiply.” Blessing on Judah
net.DEU_33_7: And this is the blessing to Judah. He said, “Listen, O Lord, to Judah’s voice, and bring him to his people. May his power be great, and may you help him against his foes.” Blessing on Levi
net.DEU_33_8: Of Levi he said: “Your Thummim and Urim belong to your godly one, whose authority you challenged at Massah, and with whom you argued at the waters of Meribah.
net.DEU_33_9: He said to his father and mother, ‘I have not seen him,’ and he did not acknowledge his own brothers or know his own children, for they kept your word, and guarded your covenant.
net.DEU_33_10: They will teach Jacob your ordinances and Israel your law; they will offer incense as a pleasant odor, and a whole offering on your altar.
net.DEU_33_11: Bless, O Lord, his goods, and be pleased with his efforts; undercut the legs of any who attack him, and of those who hate him, so that they cannot stand.” Blessing on Benjamin
net.DEU_33_12: Of Benjamin he said: “The beloved of the Lord will live safely by him; he protects him all the time, and the Lord places him on his chest.” Blessing on Joseph
net.DEU_33_13: Of Joseph he said: “May the Lord bless his land with the harvest produced by the sky, by the dew, and by the depths crouching beneath;
net.DEU_33_14: with the harvest produced by the daylight and by the moonlight;
net.DEU_33_15: with the best of the ancient mountains and the harvest produced by the age-old hills;
net.DEU_33_16: with the harvest of the earth and its fullness and the pleasure of him who resided in the burning bush. May blessing rest on Joseph’s head, and on the top of the head of the one set apart from his brothers.
net.DEU_33_17: May the firstborn of his bull bring him honor, and may his horns be those of a wild ox; with them may he gore all peoples, all the far reaches of the earth. They are the ten thousands of Ephraim, and they are the thousands of Manasseh.” Blessing on Zebulun and Issachar
net.DEU_33_18: Of Zebulun he said: “Rejoice, Zebulun, when you go outside, and Issachar, when you are in your tents.
net.DEU_33_19: They will summon peoples to the mountain, there they will sacrifice proper sacrifices; for they will enjoy the abundance of the seas, and the hidden treasures of the shores.” Blessing on Gad
net.DEU_33_20: Of Gad he said: “Blessed be the one who enlarges Gad. Like a lioness he will dwell; he will tear at an arm—indeed, a scalp.
net.DEU_33_21: He has selected the best part for himself, for the portion of the ruler is set aside there; he came with the leaders of the people, he obeyed the righteous laws of the Lord and his ordinances with Israel.” Blessing on Dan
net.DEU_33_22: Of Dan he said: “Dan is a lion’s cub; he will leap forth from Bashan.” Blessing on Naphtali
net.DEU_33_23: Of Naphtali he said: “O Naphtali, overflowing with favor, and full of the Lord’s blessing, possess the west and south.” Blessing on Asher
net.DEU_33_24: Of Asher he said: “Asher is blessed with children; may he be favored by his brothers, and may he dip his foot in olive oil.
net.DEU_33_25: The bars of your gates will be made of iron and bronze, and may you have lifelong strength.” General Praise and Blessing
net.DEU_33_26: “There is no one like God, O Jeshurun, who rides through the sky to help you, on the clouds in majesty.
net.DEU_33_27: The everlasting God is a refuge, and underneath you are his eternal arms; he has driven out enemies before you, and has said, “Destroy!”
net.DEU_33_28: Israel lives in safety, the fountain of Jacob is quite secure, in a land of grain and new wine; indeed, its heavens rain down dew.
net.DEU_33_29: You have joy, Israel! Who is like you? You are a people delivered by the Lord, your protective shield and your exalted sword. May your enemies cringe before you; may you trample on their backs.”
net.DEU_34_1: Then Moses ascended from the rift valley plains of Moab to Mount Nebo, to the summit of Pisgah, which is opposite Jericho. The Lord showed him the whole land—Gilead to Dan,
net.DEU_34_2: and all of Naphtali, the land of Ephraim and Manasseh, all the land of Judah as far as the distant sea,
net.DEU_34_3: the Negev, and the plain of the Valley of Jericho, the city of date palm trees, as far as Zoar.
net.DEU_34_4: Then the Lord said to him, “This is the land I promised to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob when I said, ‘I will give it to your descendants.’ I have let you see it, but you will not cross over there.”
net.DEU_34_5: So Moses, the servant of the Lord, died there in the land of Moab as the Lord had said.
net.DEU_34_6: He buried him in the valley in the land of Moab near Beth Peor, but no one knows his exact burial place to this very day.
net.DEU_34_7: Moses was 120 years old when he died, but his eye was not dull nor had his vitality departed.
net.DEU_34_8: The Israelites mourned for Moses in the rift valley plains of Moab for thirty days; then the days of mourning for Moses ended. An Epitaph for Moses
net.DEU_34_9: Now Joshua son of Nun was full of the spirit of wisdom, for Moses had placed his hands on him; and the Israelites listened to him and did just what the Lord had commanded Moses.
net.DEU_34_10: No prophet ever again arose in Israel like Moses, who knew the Lord face to face.
net.DEU_34_11: He did all the signs and wonders the Lord had sent him to do in the land of Egypt, to Pharaoh, all his servants, and the whole land,
net.DEU_34_12: and he displayed great power and awesome might in view of all Israel.
